mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit-sensitive demand, which compresses corporate earnings multiples and drives negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 when INDPRO and HOUST move in opposite directions, or forward returns are not significantly lower during co-declines.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising CPI inflation erodes real yields and triggers valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL accelerates alongside rising FEDFUNDS, or the signal predictive power disappears when real rates are controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly acceleration in real GDP growth expands corporate revenue expectations, directly supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, it signals robust labor demand that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS exceeds the threshold, or the signal generates false breakouts during Fed tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable industrial production indicate healthy demand-driven growth without supply bottlenecks, which supports equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 when RSAFS rises but INDPRO stagnates, or forward returns are not significantly higher during co-advances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a sustained decline in industrial production signals supply-side contraction that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines alongside elevated CPIAUCSL, or the signal predictive power disappears when real yields are controlled for.
